description:
Credibility gap: a mutation tester that reports no mutation score on its own code. `self_mutation_refused` (.spec/decisions/self_mutation_refused.md) makes in-process self-mutation impossible (the runner would crash mutating itself), so this needs a child-BEAM / external-process approach — essentially the test/integration/downstream_adoption_test.exs pattern aimed at mutagen_ex itself.

Produce a mutation score for the high-value modules (mutators/*, scope_resolver, cli, json_reporter) and publish it.

Larger effort than the other children — fine to land after the initial publish, but high-value for credibility.

Acceptance: a reproducible self-mutation score exists and is cited in the README/docs.

comments.text: ORCHESTRATOR NOTE: first implementer attempt was interrupted by a Claude spend/usage limit before it could commit or post IMPLEMENTED (infra, not a code defect). Its uncommitted scratch (a self_mutation_test.exs + self_mutation_harness.exs sketch) was saved for reference. Stage reset to the current epic HEAD (004f219, which now contains the .1/.2/.4 README sections) and a fresh --impl-only worker is being respawned on the remembers profile. This consumes attempts.impl (now 2 of 2).

IMPLEMENTED (commit 7f2478b on mutagen-bqd.3/dogfood-self-mutation)

Reproducible child-BEAM self-mutation score for mutagen_ex on its own high-value modules.

Mechanism (external-process, modeled on test/integration/downstream_adoption_test.exs):
- test/integration/support/self_mutation_harness.exs shadow-copies mutagen_ex's whole lib/ closure (minus lib/mix/tasks/) into a throwaway Mix project, mechanically rewriting the `MutagenEx`/`mutagen_ex` namespace to a `SelfMut`/`self_mut` shadow root so the self_mutation_refused guard (which only matches the literal `MutagenEx.` prefix) no longer fires. The shadow project wires the real mutagen_ex in as a path dep to supply `mix mutagen`, then the harness drives `mix mutagen --scope lib/self_mut/<m>.ex --tests <m>_test.exs` via System.cmd per target and parses the JSON report.
- The lowercase `mutagen_ex` rewrite must be global, not just the atom form: three cited tests read their own source from disk (e.g. File.read!("lib/mutagen_ex/scope_resolver.ex")) for provenance assertions; the global rewrite keeps those reads pointed at the shadow tree so the baseline stays green.

Targets + result (byte-identical across two clean runs; content-addressed IDs + serial seeded execution):
- mutators: 1 site, 1 killed (thin registry module — expected)
- scope_resolver: 60 sites, 34 killed (0.57)
- json_reporter: 18 sites, 11 killed (0.61)
- AGGREGATE: 79 sites, 46 killed, kill rate 0.58

`cli` is intentionally excluded (documented in the harness): cli_test.exs is half Mix.Tasks.Mutagen.run/2 tests that bind to the excluded Mix task and the literal `MutagenEx.` guard prefix; two of them can never pass under the shadow rewrite (a `%SelfMut.Config{}` vs real `%MutagenEx.Config{}` expectation, and `SelfMut.MutationRunner` not being refused). Both redden the baseline before any mutation runs and neither exercises SelfMut.Cli. CLI parse logic is covered indirectly via scope_resolver, which consumes the scopes cli.ex parses.

Acceptance — reproducible self-mutation score cited in README: README "## Efficacy on real codebases" gains a "### Dogfood: mutagen_ex on its own source" subsection citing 46/79 (0.58), composing with the existing self-study/cross-project sections (not duplicating them).

Wiring: test tagged :self_mutation, excluded from default `mix test` (test/test_helper.exs), included in the `mix test.integration` alias (mix.exs). Run via `mix test --include self_mutation`.

Verification (Elixir 1.19.5):
- mix compile --warnings-as-errors: clean
- mix test --seed 0: 640 tests, 0 failures (20 excluded) — default lane unaffected
- mix test test/integration/self_mutation_test.exs --include self_mutation --seed 0: 1 test, 0 failures (13.7s)

title: Dogfood: child-BEAM self-mutation score
